name: Cebiche De Corvina
recipeIngredient: 1 lb flounder or sole filets, the freshest you can find
aji or chili or Tabasco - to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the filets in water and plenty of salt three times.
2. Rinse the filets again in unsalted water.
3. Using a very sharp knife, cut strips of fish measuring 1 inch by 1/4 inch at an angle.
4. Squeeze the juice of 1 lime into the bottom of a small Pyrex dish to ensure it is evenly coated.
5. Place the strips of fish side by side in the dish so they lie comfortably.
6. Season with salt more than usual, but avoid overdoing it.
7. Sprinkle a little freshly ground white pepper.
8. Squeeze lemon or lime juice over the top, ensuring it almost covers the fish. Use your hands to distribute the citrus; do not squeeze too hard to prevent bitterness from the lemon.
9. Cut the remaining fish and place an additional layer in the Pyrex dish.
10. Repeat steps: season with salt, pepper, and lemon juice until all fish is immersed.
11. Thinly slice aji or chili (remove seeds) and place them skin side up on top of the fish. The amount should be as hot as desired; add a little Tabasco for extra heat.
12. Slice the red onion very fine, almost transparent. Separate it from the rest of the ingredients.
13. Spread the sliced onions over the fish to cover all pieces.
14. Sprinkle salt, white pepper, and lemon juice on top. Ensure the fish is fully immersed in citrus; add more if necessary.
15. Squeeze the juice of half an orange over the top.
16. If desired, sprinkle chopped parsley (preferably just before serving).
17. Gently shake the dish to mix the flavors evenly.
18. Let stand covered at room temperature for 3 hours or until opaque and firm. Chill until ready to serve. Serve over lettuce leaves.
